Software Review

Knowify Review (2026)

Built specifically for commercial subcontractors. Here's what Knowify does — and who it's actually right for.

⭐ Rating: 4.1/5 — Best for commercial/project-based subcontractors

Knowify is a construction project management platform purpose-built for commercial and project-based subcontractors. While platforms like Buildertrend dominate the residential space and Procore owns the enterprise commercial market, Knowify targets the gap: specialty subcontractors doing commercial work who need AIA billing, contract management, and real job costing without Procore's complexity or price.

Who Knowify is designed for

Knowify is primarily marketed to commercial subcontractors — electrical, mechanical, HVAC, plumbing, fire protection, and similar specialty trades doing project-based work with GCs. If you bill by application for payment (G702/G703 AIA format), manage subcontracts with your own subs, and track costs against a bid, Knowify was designed with you in mind.

Pricing

PlanPriceKey Features
Starter ~$149/mo Project management, basic job costing, invoicing
Pro ~$299/mo Adds AIA billing, change order management, subcontract management
Enterprise Custom Multi-location, advanced reporting, API access

Note: Pricing shown is approximate and may vary. Contact Knowify for a current quote — they sometimes offer adjusted pricing based on company size and trade.

Core features

AIA billing — The standout feature

Knowify's AIA billing module generates G702 and G703 applications for payment in the format GCs and owners actually want. This is genuinely undervalued — for commercial subcontractors, producing accurate pay apps in the right format and getting them submitted on time is critical to cash flow. Knowify automates the calculation of completed work, stored materials, retainage, and net payment due.

  • G702/G703 generation with all standard fields populated automatically
  • Retainage tracking on both schedule of values lines and in aggregate
  • Change order integration — CO amounts flow into the SoV automatically when approved
  • Payment tracking through the lien waiver exchange cycle

Job costing

Knowify tracks costs against your original bid in real time. Labor hours, material purchases, sub costs, and equipment are all tracked against the project budget with variance reporting. For subcontractors bidding competitive commercial work, this visibility is essential — you need to know if you're making money on each job type to sharpen future bids.

Contract management

Knowify manages your subcontract documents — prime contracts from GCs, your own subcontracts to lower-tier subs — with status tracking, expiration alerts, and document storage. Combined with lien waiver management, this helps commercial subs stay compliant with contract requirements.

Change order management

Change order workflows in Knowify track CO requests through submission, GC review, approval, and integration into the schedule of values. This is more complete than most residential-focused platforms' change order tools, which are designed for homeowner-facing change orders rather than commercial GC interactions.

Scheduling

Knowify includes basic Gantt scheduling. It's functional but not the platform's strength — it's adequate for scheduling your own crew's work but not as visual or feature-rich as Buildertrend's scheduling tools.

What Knowify doesn't do well

  • Not designed for residential work — client portal and homeowner-facing features are minimal
  • Estimating is basic compared to dedicated estimating tools or UDA ConstructionOnline
  • No built-in lead generation or CRM
  • Mobile app less polished than market leaders
  • Smaller user community means fewer third-party resources and integrations

Knowify vs. the competition

FeatureKnowifyProcoreBuildertrendJobTread
AIA billing⭐ Excellent⭐ ExcellentNot includedLimited
Commercial sub focus⭐ Core focusBroadResidentialAny trade
Price$149–299/moEnterprise$499–1,099/mo$99/mo
Residential usePoorPossible⭐ ExcellentGood
Ease of useModerateComplex⭐ Good⭐ Good

The verdict

Knowify serves a real and underserved market. Commercial subcontractors billing via AIA applications, managing complex change orders with GCs, and tracking job costs against competitive bids will find Knowify purpose-built for their workflow in a way that residential platforms simply aren't.

The AIA billing module alone can pay for the platform in time savings for a sub doing 10+ payment applications per month. If you're currently producing G702/G703 forms in Excel or Word and manually calculating retainage, that pain is real and Knowify solves it.

For residential contractors: look at Buildertrend or Houzz Pro instead. For enterprise commercial work: Procore is the standard. But for mid-market commercial subs who live between those two worlds — Knowify is worth a serious look.

For residential contractors, Buildertrend and Houzz Pro are the market leaders:

Try Buildertrend Try Houzz Pro Free
